original: St Agapitus I begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Pope Boniface I turns the Pantheon in Rome into a Catholic church
Eligius (Saint Eloy) becomes bishop of Doornik-Noyon
Henry I of Limburg loses the duchy of Lower Lorraine to Godfrey of Louvain
Crusaders march into Beirut causing a bloodbath
As Vizier Mehmet I of Karaman issues a firman (decree) ordering only the Turkish language to be used, not Arabic or Persian
Peter Coutherel banished from Leuven
Pope Alexander VI excommunicates Italian Dominican friar Girolamo Savonarola for leading the expulsion of the Medici's from Florence
Exhumed corpse of heretic David Jorisz burned in Basel
King Henri III flees Paris
Dutch Admiral Hermites' Nassau fleet blockades Lima, Peru
Cardinal Richelieu of France reputedly creates the table knife
Construction of the Red Fort begins at Shahjahanabad, Mughal Empire (now Dehli, India)
Battle of Grantham: English parliamentary armies beat royalists
Heavy earthquake strikes Santiago, Chile; kills a third of the population
Ingen Ryuki invited to become the abbot of Sofokuji temple in Nagasaki
Venetian fleet under Norwegian Admiral Cort Adeler beats Turkish fleet the near the Dardanelles, sinking 15 Turkish galleys
University library at Vienna opens
War of Bavarian Succession ends
The Republic of Ecuador is founded with Juan José Flores as president
First performance of Finland's national anthem "Maamme", composed by Fredrik Pacius, Swedish words by Johan Ludvig Runeberg
The Great Comet of 1861 is discovered by John Tebbutt of Windsor, New South Wales, Australia
-16] Atlanta Campaign: Battle of Resaca, Georgia
Battle of Palmito Ranch, near Brownsville, Texas: final engagement of the American Civil War, Private John Jefferson Williams of B Company, 34th Regiment Indiana Infantry is last man killed
Pope Pius IX encyclical "On Greek-Ruthenian rite"
Amersfoort-Zutphen railway in the Netherlands opens
Caesar Franck's symphonic poem "Lesson Eolides" premieres

Danvers State Hospital, a psychiatric hospital in Massachusetts, opens and later serves as inspiration for Arkham Sanitorium in the work of H.P. Lovecraft, which in turn inspires Arkham Asylum of the D.C. Batman universe
Toba indians kill 20 members of French expedition in Argentina, including explorer Jules Nicolas Crevaux
Institute for Electrical & Electronics Engineers (IEEE) forms in New York
DeWolf Hopper first recites the comedic baseball poem "Casey at the Bat"
Princess Isabel of Brazil acting as regent signs the "Lei Auréa" (Golden Law) abolishing slavery in Brazil
Bezalel Art School opens in Jerusalem
Christian National Labor Workers (CNV) party begins in Netherlands
First Giro d'Italia takes place in Milan - Italian cyclist Luigi Ganna is the winner
NY Giant Fred Merkle is 1st to get 6 RBIs in an inning (1st)
Royal Flying Corps forms in Great Britain

US Secretary of State Bryan sends a note to Germany demanding that Germany disavow the attacks on the Lusitania and make immediate reparations; however, the note is written only to 'pacify exited public opinion', according to Bryan
1st observance of Indian (Native American) Day
Lafayette Escadrille, an American air force unit under French command comprised of volunteers to fight for France, see first combat at the Battle of Verdun
1st appearance of Mary to 3 shepherd children in Fatima, Portugal
First US airmail stamps are issued (24 cents)
Pulitzer Prize awarded to Willa Cather for her World War I-based novel "One of Ours"
"Black Friday" on Berlin Stock Exchange
VVOG soccer team forms in Harderwijk
Paul Doumer elected President of France
Quiroga government takes office in Spain
Aviation pioneerIgor Sikorskypilots first untethered test flight of his VS-300 helicopter in Stratford, Connecticut
British bomb factory at Breda, Netherlands
German breakthrough at Grebbelinie
Trial against resistance fighter comte d'Estienne d'Orves begins
Willy Lewis' US jazz band performs in Switzerland
Helicopter makes its 1st cross-country flight
Pitcher Jim Tobin belts 3 HRs in a game
German & Italian forces in Africa surrender
German occupiers attempt to confiscate all radios in the Netherlands
US troops conquer Dakeshi Okinawa
Sarwate & Banerjee add 249 for 10th wkt for Indians v Surrey
US sentences 58 camp guards of Mauthausen concentration camp to death
Winston Churchillwelcomed in Rotterdam
US Senate approves the Taft-Hartley Act limiting the power of unions
First British-produced jet bomber, the Canberra, makes its first test flight
Diner's Club issues its 1st credit cards
First-ever race of the Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship is run at Silverstone, England, and won by Giuseppe Farina of Italy in an Alfa Romeo
Ron Necciai of Pittsburgh Pirate's Bristol Twins Class D farm team, strikes out 27 in 9-innings, as he no-hits Welch Minors; 4 Minors do reach base
The Rajya Sabha, the upper house of the Parliament of India, holds its first sitting
Chinese Middle School students in Singapore take part in anti-National Service riots
Labour Party wins British municipal elections
Robin Roberts gives up a HR then retires next 27 men in a row
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Enewetak Atoll
French settlers riot against French army in Algeria
Jordan and Iraq form the Arab Federation
MLB San Francisco Giants teammatesWillie Mays& Darryl Spencer each hit 2 HRs & a triple, and combine for 10 RBI in a 16-9 win over the Dodgers at Los Angeles
Pierre Pflimlin forms French government
The trade mark Velcro is registered
1st launch of Delta satellite launching vehicle; it failed
Phillies lose 3rd consecutive 1-0 game
WOLE TV channel 12 in Aguadillo, PR
Ossie Davis' comedic stage farce "Purlie Victorious", starring the playwright, closes the Longacre, Theatre, NYC, after 261 performances
Several Arab nations break ties with West Germany after it establishes diplomatic relations with Israel
US Federal education funding is denied to 12 school districts in the South because of violations of the 1964 Civil Rights Act
Dutch counterculture movement Provo disbands
Octagonal boxing ring is tested to avoid corner injuries
Zakir Husain is elected the third President of India
Race riots, later known as the May 13 Incident, take place in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
Grace Slick of Jefferson Airplane seriously injured in a car accident
115 die in nightclub atop 7-story Sennichi department store (Osaka Japan)
Battle at Springmartin: following a loyalist car bombing of a Catholic-owned pub in the Ballymurphy area of Belfast, clashes erupt between PIRA, UVF and British Army
Milwaukee Brewers beat Minnesota Twins, 4-3, in 22 innings (started 5/12)
Hail stones as large as tennis balls hit Wernerville, Tennessee
Henry Rono of Kenya sets record for 3,000 m steeplechase (8:05.4)
Joie Chitwood drives a Chevette 5.6 miles on just 2 wheels
"Utter Glory... Morrissey Hall" opens & closes at Mark Hellinger NYC
Michael Hilland his wife, Christine, open their first Michael Hill Jeweller store in Whangārei, New Zealand
Cincinnati Red Ray Knight hits 2 HRs in 5th inning vs NY Mets
Braniff Airlines files for bankruptcy
Chicago Cubs win their 8,000th (beat Astros)
Soyuz T-5 is launched-Berezovoi & Lebedev for 211 days in space
Carlton Fisk becomes 5th catcher to steal 100 bases

Philadelphia, Pennsylvania police drop explosive device on house occupied by revolutionary Black separatist group "Move", killing 11, during stand-off after a shoot-out; ensuing fire destroys 61 homes
Trinidad & Tobago ties US 1-1, in 3rd round of 1990 world soccer cup
Apple releases Macintosh operating System 7, codenamed the "Big Bang"
Concrete foundation for ballpark at Jacobs Field (now Progressive Field) is poured, In downtown Cleveland, Ohio
Li Hongzhi gives the first public lecture on Falun Gong in Changchun, China
Three astronauts simultaneously walk in space for the first time
CBS' soap opera "Knots Landing" ends 14 year-run with 334th show in Netherlands and US
Methane gas explosion in Secunda coal mine South-Africa, kills 50
MLB Cleveland Indians begin a 18 home game win streak at newly opened Jacobs Field
6.5 earthquake hits Greece
Severe thunderstorms and a tornado in Bangladesh kill an estimated 600 people
India carries out two additional nuclear tests at Pokhran after the three conducted on May 11, resulting in the US and Japan imposing economic sanctions
Race riots break out in Jakarta, Indonesia, where shops owned by Indonesians of Chinese descent are looted and women raped
Carlo Azeglio Ciampi is elected 10th President of Italy
In Enschede, the Netherlands, a fireworks factory explodes, killing 22 people, wounding 950, and resulting in approximately €450 million in damage
The final episode of "Frasier" on NBC is watched by 33 million people
The Andijan Massacre occurs in Uzbekistan
5th Republic protest in support of state secularism held in İzmir, Turkey
At 16 years, 65 days Matthew Briggs debuts for Fulham in a 3-1 defeat at Middlesbrough; youngest player to appear in an English Premier League match
Construction of the Calafat-Vidin Bridge between Romania and Bulgaria begins
Portugal slips into double-dip recession after the economy contracts by 0.7% in the first quarter of 2011, with a 0.6% contraction in the last quarter of 2010, it is reported today
Romania officially leaves the recession after 2 years following economic growth of an annualised 1.6% in the first quarter
49 dismembered bodies are found on a Mexican highway as part of the Mexican drug war

Manchester City beats QPR 3-2 at City of Manchester Stadium to win English Premier League title on goal difference from Manchester United; Citizens recover from 2–1 down with 2 stoppage-time goals; first EPL title decided on goal difference
Manchester City win the English Premier League for the first time
Torrential rain in Hunan Province, China, destroys a bridge, 3,500 homes and displaces 28,000 people
301 miners are killed by an underground explosion and mine fire in Soma Mine, Manisa, Turkey
Unidentified shipwreck is discovered off the northern coast of Haiti by marine archaeologist Barry Clifford
22-year-old UK blogger halts the spread of a global ransomware cyberattack by accidentally identifying a "kill switch"
Bus plunges off cliff near Marmaris, Turkey, killing at least 23, injuring 11
Arsène Wenger guides Arsenal to a 1-0 win at Huddersfield in his final game as Gunners manager after 22 years in charge
China's first domestically built aircraft carrier begins sea trials in Dalian, China
Family of six carry out 3 church bombings in Surabaya, Indonesia, killing at least 13. Islamic State claims responsibility.
Manchester City ends the EPL season with a 1-0 win at Southampton having smashed 11 Premier League records including biggest title winning margin (19 points) and most points (100)
China retaliates against the US as their trade war escalates, raising tariffs on $60 billion worth of goods affecting over 4,000 items
Diary of Dutch statesman Johan van Oldenbarnevelt's last days rediscovered and displayed for the 400th anniversary of his execution
New study on the moon shows it is still shrinking with recent moonquakes as it cools, published in journal "Nature Geoscience"
Sri Lanka imposes a nationwide curfew from 9pm to 4am to curb violence against Muslims after the Easter Sunday terrorist attacks
Every African country records cases of COVID-19 as The Kingdom of Lesotho becomes the last country on the continent to record a case
American CDC says people fully vaccinated against COVID-19 can stop wearing masks

Gaza conflict has killed 103 people in Palestine, including 27 children, through Israeli airstrikes, more than 1000 rockets fired into Israel by Gaza militants killing seven, city of Lod center of street fighting
World's largest iceberg 'A-76' at 1,667-square-miles (4,320 square km) calves off the Ronne Ice Shelf in Antarctica
MaryQueen of Scotsis defeated at Battle of Langside
English colonists led byJohn Smithestablish Jamestown at a second landing near the James River in Virginia - first permanent English settlement in North America
Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's first opera "Apollo et Hyacinthus", written when he was 11 years old, premieres in Salzburg
US Congress votes in favor of PresidentJames K. Polk’s request to declare war on Mexico over border disputes
Queen Victoriaannounces Britain's position of neutrality during the US Civil War
British PMLord Salisburyoffers Heligoland islands to Germany in exchange for Zanzibar, Uganda & Equatoria (becomes Heligoland–Zanzibar Treaty that July)
World heavyweight boxing championJames J. Jeffriesretires undefeated after 7 title defences; returns in 1910 to be beaten by Jack Johnson
First flight of a four-engined aircraft, the Russky Vityaz, designed byIgor Sikorskyin Russia
DutchQueen Wilhelminaflees to England
Winston Churchillsays "I have nothing to offer but blood, toil, tears and sweat" in his first speech as Prime Minister to the British House of Commons
Martin Bormannis named head of Nazi Chancellery in Germany
Jawaharlal Nehrubecomes premier of India
Mickey Mantlehits 3 consecutive HRs of at least 463'
MLB playerStan Musialis 8th to get 3,000 hits
Kraft Music Hall withMilton Berlelast airs on NBC-TV
The Rolling Stonesrelease "Paint it Black" single in the UK
1,000,000 French demonstrate againstCharles De Gaulle&Georges Pompidou
Bobby RiggsbeatsMargaret Smith Courtin Mother's Day match in California
Howard Sternbegins his professional broadcasting career at WRNW radio in Briarcliff Manor, New York
Shah of IranMohammad Reza Pahlaviand family sentenced to death in Tehran
PopeJohn Paul IIis shot and critically wounded by Turkish gunmanMehmet Ali Ağcain St Peter's Square, Vatican City
Reggie Jacksonis 1st major leaguer to strike out 2,000 times
Dutch footballerJohan Cruyff's last competitive match
Approximately 2,000 students begin a hunger strike in Tiananmen Square, China, to force a dialogue with the government ahead of a visit by Soviet leaderMikhail Gorbachev
Minnesota TwinKirby Puckettbecomes 35th to hit 4 doubles in a game
"Michael Jackson: Magic & Madness" goes on sale
Yankee Stadium fans sing "Like a Virgin" to JoseCanseco
Frank Stallone beatsGeraldo Riverain a boxing bout on Howard Stern Show
Arsenio Hall's 1,000th show retrospective seen in Netherlands
KC RoyalGeorge Bretthits his 300th HR
Team New Zealandbeats Team Dennis Conner 5-0 to win the America's Cup for the first time
O.J. Simpsonappears on British TV discussing his not guilty verdict
Eddie Murrayis 6th baseball player to play in 3,000 games
Silvio Berlusconi's right-wing "House of Freedoms" coalition wins the Italian general election
Jason Mrazreleases his third studio album, "We Sing. We Dance. We Steal Things."
Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Elton John,Bruce Springsteen,Mary J. Blige,Debbie Harry,Lady Gaga,Shirley Bassey, Nile Rodgers and Chic
Canadian astronautChris Hadfielddeparts the International Space Station to return to Earth
Chance the Rapperreleases his third mixtape "Coloring Book" on Apple Music - first album to chart on Billboard Top 100 based only on streams (Grammy for Best Rap Album)
Portuguese children Francisco and Jacinta Marto (died 1919 & 1920 aged 10 & 9 in flu epidemic) canonized byPope Francisat Fátima - the youngest Catholic saints not to die as martyrs
Liverpool's Egyptian soccer forwardMohamed Salahscores in a 4-0 win against Brighton to set the EPL goal scoring record (32) for a 38-game season
ActressFelicity Huffmanpleads guilty for her part in the US college admissions scandal
The Tradition Senior Men's Golf, Greystone G &CC: Steve Stricker wins his first career major title by 6 strokes ahead of Billy Andrade, Paul Goydos &David Toms
Kendrick Lamar's diss track "Not Like Us" becomes his 4th #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart

Commissioner Rob Manfred removes 17 deceased people, including "Shoeless" Joe Jackson andPete Rosefrom MLB's 'permanently ineligible' list stating, “Obviously, a person no longer with us cannot represent a threat to the integrity of the game,” paving the way for HOF inclusion
The First Fleet, headed byArthur Phillip, sets sail with 11 ships of convicts for Botany Bay, Australia
Former President of SudanOmar al-Bashiris charged in connection with the deaths of protesters the previous month
